tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment with metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals protein, glucose, ketones, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Low HbA1c is unusual and may reflect anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for possible chronic kidney disease and underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
